calculated_metrics.confidence_note: This unit cost is estimated by PRISM by dividing total programme expenditure by the total quantified animal beneficiaries.

details.calculation: ($163,416 USD / 4,310 total beneficiaries) = $37.92 USD per outcome. | A $1,000 USD donation achieves ≈ 26 outcomes.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

details.elaboration: ["Quote: This year, the Darjeeling Animal Shelter successfully vaccinated 2648 dogs against rabies across all 32 wards, marking a significant achievement in its ongoing efforts to create a safe environment for all.", "Highest tractability intervention found: 'Policy And Legal Advocacy'", "EA Rationale: Policy and legal advocacy can create systemic, legally-binding protections for animals, representing a highly tractable path to impact."]
details.formula: Mapping of charity's intervention types against the EA Intervention Tractability evidence base.

beneficiaries.location: Kalimpong and Darjeeling, West Bengal, India

metrics.context_qualifier: Total dogs and cats vaccinated against rabies across Kalimpong and Darjeeling districts during the reporting year.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Not reported. Without intervention, rabies would continue to spread, leading to animal suffering and potential human deaths.

metrics.evidence_quote: Anti-Rabies Vaccination (Dogs & Cats) Total = 4310 (In 12 months) April 2023 To March 2024

metrics.context_qualifier: Total clinical treatments provided to small animals, birds, pigs, and calves at Kalimpong and Darjeeling shelters.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Not reported. Without intervention, sick and injured animals would suffer or die without medical care.

metrics.evidence_quote: This year, Kalimpong Animal Shelter treated 5054 animals, while Darjeeling Animal Shelter, operating as its sister unit, treated over 3368 animals, giving them renewed chances at life.

metrics.context_qualifier: Total dogs (1259) and cats (316) sterilised through the Animal Birth Control (ABC) Programme in Kalimpong and Darjeeling.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Not reported. Without intervention, stray dog and cat populations would increase, leading to more suffering and inhumane culling practices.

metrics.evidence_quote: Kalimpong Animal Shelter and Darjeeling Animal Shelter made significant strides in animal welfare, sterilising 1259 dogs and 316 cats and vaccinating 4310 against rabies.

metrics.context_qualifier: Total dogs rescued by Kalimpong Animal Shelter and Darjeeling Animal Shelter during the reporting year.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Not reported. Without intervention, these animals would have continued to suffer or died from their injuries/circumstances.

metrics.evidence_quote: In 2023, the sister concerns of DGAS - Kalimpong Animal Shelter & Darjeeling Animal Shelter rescued a total of 612 dogs.

metrics.context_qualifier: Total puppies (160) and kittens (33) successfully adopted from DGAS shelters into loving forever homes.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Not reported. Without intervention, these animals would remain in the shelter or face an uncertain future.

metrics.evidence_quote: This year, DGAS successfully found loving forever homes to 160 puppies and 33 kittens, giving them a second chance at life.

significant_events.event_name: Anti-Rabies Vaccination Camp / DAS Rabies Project
significant_events.source_quote: This year, the Darjeeling Animal Shelter successfully vaccinated 2648 dogs against rabies across all 32 wards, marking a significant achievement in its ongoing efforts to create a safe environment for all.
significant_events.summary: Extensive vaccination drives across 32 wards of Darjeeling, focusing on stray and community dogs, with support from Dogs Trust Worldwide and Mission Rabies. Achieved 75% vaccination coverage in Darjeeling.

significant_events.source_quote: Since 1998, the Kalimpong Animal Shelter has been instrumental in managing the stray dog population in Kalimpong and controlling rabies through its Animal Birth Control (ABC) and Anti-Rabies Vaccination Programmes.
significant_events.summary: Ongoing program since 1998 (KAS) and 2008 (DAS) to manage stray dog population and control rabies through catch, spay/neuter, and release, following WHO guidelines.

significant_events.event_name: Disaster Relief for Teesta Flash Floods
significant_events.source_quote: In the wake of this disaster, the Kalimpong Animal Shelter team, as the first animal responders was on the ground providing rapid aid, comfort, and support to these vulnerable animals.
significant_events.summary: Provided rapid aid, comfort, and support to animals in distress (including rescues, rehabilitation, food, medical care, vaccinations) after the Glacial Lake Outburst Flood in October 2023.

significant_events.source_quote: In 2023, Kalimpong Animal Shelter, in collaboration with Help Animals India, saved the lives of 300 community dogs in remote rural areas of Kalimpong through the Village Camp Programme.
significant_events.summary: Outreach initiative in remote rural areas of Kalimpong, providing sterilisation and rabies vaccinations to 300 community dogs, fostering community participation.

significant_events.source_quote: To address these challenges, Kalimpong Animal Shelter, supported by the Marchig Animal Welfare Trust, launched a spay and neuter campaign this year, aimed at helping felines lead healthy and stress-free lives.
significant_events.summary: Launched by Kalimpong Animal Shelter, supported by Marchig Animal Welfare Trust, to address growing feline population, cruelty, abandonment, and accidents.

significant_events.source_quote: For the first time, Darjeeling now has its own SPCA, dedicated to tackling these critical issues and advancing animal welfare throughout the region.
significant_events.summary: For the first time, Darjeeling has its own SPCA, established with support from Humane Society International (HSI) India, to tackle animal cruelty and advance welfare.

significant_events.source_quote: Our Humane Education program engages children, promoting values of kindness and respect for animals.
significant_events.summary: Program engaging children to promote kindness and respect for animals, teaching about caring for pets, rabies prevention, and dog bites. Includes shelter tours.

significant_events.event_name: First-Aid Training Programme for Small Animals
significant_events.source_quote: On August 23, 2023, the Darjeeling Animal Shelter (DAS) and Humane Society International (HSI) jointly organised a first aid training session for small animals at the Animal Resources Department GTA in Darjeeling, drawing over 50 animal enthusiasts and advocates from Darjeeling and neighbouring towns.
significant_events.summary: Jointly organised with HSI, training session for over 50 animal enthusiasts and advocates to empower them with knowledge for on-the-spot treatment and first aid for injured animals.

data.original_text: Founded by Dr. Christine Townend in the year 1992 for the sole purpose of Animal Birth Control of street dogs that were suffering with genital problems , skin conditions and overburdened with a litter of puppies that were neither adopted nor fostered.
The female dogs and litter of puppies had to succumb a life of despair and cruelty because the locals feared of rabies and female dogs procreating time and again.
About 22 years ago, rabies, a fatal disease/virus was on the rise in India and in these two Indian districts under West Bengal, Kalimpong and Darjeeling.

The Municipality unanimously agreed to this approach then a new hope of positivity dawned on Dr. Christine by starting an animal shelter with a dispensary set-up for the near future well-being of street dogs where animals could be treated, rescued, caught for sterilisation and sheltered post-operative care for a few days and then be released.
With this, Kalimpong Animal Shelter enacted the ABC Programme in 1998 and a few years later, the same Programme was extended to Darjeeling Goodwill Animal Shelter, a sister shelter.
Dog creche providing healthcare, sterilization, and vaccination services to improve the lives of street dogs.
The shelter started an Animal Birth Control (ABC) program, focusing on spaying and vaccinating dogs.
The staff faced challenges in identifying stray dogs from owned ones, leading to an education campaign to locate dog owners and promote the benefits of spaying.
Over the years so many dogs have been spayed and vaccinated that, not only has the health of the community dog population greatly increased, but the population of puppies has been greatly reduced.
These days there are so few puppies available in Kalimpong town, that Darjeeling Animal Shelter sends surplus pups from Darjeeling to KAS for adoption.
In addition to the ABC programme, the staff at KAS conducted many camps in far-lying villages only accessible by walking.
KAS has saved the lives of thousands of animals since its foundation in 1993.
Due to the work of DAS and KAS, together with the Darjeeling and Kalimpong Municipalities, all poisoning of street dogs has stopped from 1998 onwards.
The street dogs are now safe and friendly.
Puppies are not being born to die an early death from starvation and disease.

The Chairman of both Darjeeling and Kalimpong Municipalities agreed to stop poisoning all dogs and since then KAS and DAS have successfully carried out ABC programmes. No further dogs in either Municipality have been poisoned.
